perturbation.original_text: PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT AGREES that all television shows, DVD Magazines, Internet Streaming Video Television Shows, Television Shorts, Radio Shows, Radio Shorts, Cell Phone Video Clips, Caller ID Video Clips, Promotional Commercials, Websites, Streaming Video Commercials, Streaming Video Highlight Shows, 24 Hour Nostalgia Sports Network, DVD Program Package, Television Show Series, stock footage library, and print promotional posters, created and or produced with any content provided by PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT are wholly owned by THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION.
perturbation.changed_text: PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT AGREES that all television shows, DVD Magazines, Internet Streaming Video Television Shows, Television Shorts, Radio Shows, Radio Shorts, Cell Phone Video Clips, Caller ID Video Clips, Promotional Commercials, Websites, Streaming Video Commercials, Streaming Video Highlight Shows, 24 Hour Nostalgia Sports Network, DVD Program Package, Television Show Series, stock footage library, and print promotional posters, created and or produced with any content provided by PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT are jointly owned by THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION and PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the ownership agreement for content created using PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT's content. Initially, all such content was wholly owned by THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION. Now, it is stated that it is jointly owned. This contradicts the later definition under EXHIBIT A defining 'PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT Content' as materials 'owned by PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT'. The contradiction lies in whether content using PACIFICAP's materials is wholly owned by one party or jointly owned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT Content” shall mean, collectively, all materials, data, and similar information collected and owned by PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT, which is a collection of television shows, clips, movies, recordings and photos.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the ownership of content created using PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT's materials from 'wholly owned by THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION' to 'jointly owned by THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION and PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT'. This directly contradicts the definition of 'PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT Content' as materials 'owned by PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT'. The clear contradiction lies in whether the content is wholly owned or jointly owned, creating a significant inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION, not fulfilling this minimum PERFORMANCE, PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT may cancel this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION, not fulfilling this minimum PERFORMANCE, PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT may, at their discretion, renegotiate terms or potentially cancel this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement provides a clear consequence (cancellation) if the HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION doesn't meet the performance threshold. The modified version introduces an alternative option of renegotiation, making the consequence of non-performance less certain. This creates an in-text contradiction if another part of the contract implies or states that failing to meet performance standards automatically leads to termination. This change contradicts the 'TERMINATION FOR CAUSE' section that states the agreement may be terminated if a party breaches its obligations under the agreement. The addition of 'renegotiate terms' contradicts the right to terminate based on failure to meet performance standards.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMINATION FOR CAUSE: Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement may be terminated by either party immediately upon notice if the other party: (w) becomes insolvent; (x) files a petition in bankruptcy; (y) makes an assignment for the benefit of its creditors; or (z) breach any of its obligations under this Agreement in any material respect, which breach is not remedied within thirty (30) days following written notice to such party.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an alternative to termination (renegotiation), contradicting the 'TERMINATION FOR CAUSE' section which states the agreement 'may be terminated' for breach of obligations. This creates an in-text contradiction because it makes the consequence of non-performance less certain and therefore contradicts the original automatic right to terminate for cause if not remedied within 30 days, fitting the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.
